locations.name: Dining Room
locations.description: The formal dining room where dinner takes place and the Executor’s body is later discovered.
clues: Body of the Executor.
Original Black Envelope passed around before the blackout.
Cow bell mounted on the wall with the missing clapper.
Wax smear or ribbon near the terrace window.

locations.name: Media Room
locations.description: An adjacent room filled with legal briefs, files, and personal documents. It is the motive vault.
clues: Succession briefing for the Son.
Hostile email from the Director about the Son.
Estrangement papers for the Widow.
Crisis statement from the Publicist.
Paternity file for the Bastard Child.
Foundation audit for the Philanthropist.
Planning file for the Minister.
Provenance file for the Antique Hunter.
Opera file for the Opera Singer.
Archive transfer letter for the Daughter.

locations.name: Kitchen
locations.description: Service area where the house staff prepare and clean. Contains clues to the bell code.
clues: Bell clapper hidden under a tea towel or in the sink.
Draft note with misspelled 'rign' crossed out, hinting at the ring signal.
Written instruction to The Maid not to open the wing until the correct signal.
Tea service items indicating one ring for drinks.

locations.name: Terrace
locations.description: The patio outside the dining wing. It has a cracked window and access to the garden paths.
clues: Cracked terrace window with glass fragments.
Marks on the doorframe where a crowbar was used.
Fake black envelope thrown into the dining room.
Footprints leading toward the rose bed.

locations.name: Rose Garden
locations.description: A formal rose garden adjacent to the terrace. Has neat beds, gravel paths, and hedges.
clues: Fake black envelope may be found among the roses or gravel.
Broken stem or trampled area indicating someone retrieved or threw something.
Subtle drag marks leading toward the statue walk.

locations.name: Garden Shed
locations.description: Storage for tools, pots, and supplies. Could hide implements or clues.
clues: Crowbar storage area showing one tool missing.
Mud on the floor suggesting recent use.
Gardening gloves with traces of wax.

locations.name: Greenhouse
locations.description: Glasshouse full of exotic plants. Ventilation ducts link to the manor.
clues: Disturbed vent covers indicating attempted access to the dining wing.
Soil disturbance and broken pots where someone hid or crawled.
Unusual footprints leading back to the statue walk.

locations.name: Pond/Fountains
locations.description: Small ponds and fountains providing tranquil ambience.
clues: Water disturbed recently as if someone waded through.
A torn piece of ribbon floating near the edge.
Reflection of the Blind Judge statue reveals a loose plaque.

locations.name: Statue Walk
locations.description: Walk lined with historical statues and plaques detailing Baroo heritage.
clues: One plaque misaligned, pointing toward The Blind Judge.
Scrapes on the ground where something was dragged.
Garden soil disturbed at the base of several statues.

locations.name: The Blind Judge Statue
locations.description: Eighteenth‑century statue with a blindfold and scales. A loose plaque covers a hollow space.
clues: Hidden compartment behind the plaque containing the real Black Envelope.
Fingerprints or wax residue around the edge of the plaque.
Marks where someone prised the plaque open.

locations.name: Living Room
locations.description: Comfortable drawing room where the Police gather everyone to summarise events and read the codicil.
clues: Comparison of fake and real codicils.
Statements from each character detailing motives and accusations.
Police report consolidating evidence from Acts 1 and 2.
